skill.name: azure-ai-agents-persistent-dotnet
skill.description: Guidance for using the Azure AI Agents Persistent SDK for .NET to create agents, manage threads, stream runs, call tools, and connect file search or Azure services.
skill.summary: Build persistent Azure AI agents in .NET with threads, runs, tools, streaming, and cleanup patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review shows these are Markdown code fences, inline SDK identifiers, and documentation examples. The remaining low risk is that the skill documents commands, Azure service endpoints, and external Microsoft or NuGet links that users may choose to run or open.

low_findings.title: Documented Command Examples Require User Judgment
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives for Ruby backtick execution. The file contains Markdown code fences and package installation commands for .NET dependencies, which are documentation examples rather than automatically executed code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown or inline package install examples. No script file, shell wrapper, or automatic execution path was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings do not reference cryptographic code. The cited lines are the skill description, a weather tool description, a location parameter, and a streaming update table header.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content contains no hash functions, ciphers, keys, or cryptographic API calls. The static labels appear to be token-level matches without semantic support.

low_findings.title: External Service URLs Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: The network findings are hardcoded Azure endpoint placeholders and reference links to NuGet, Microsoft Learn, and Azure SDK GitHub pages. They are expected for SDK documentation and do not exfiltrate data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation references and placeholders. I found no hidden network call, credential collection, or unauthorized request logic.

content.user_title: Build Persistent Azure AI Agents in .NET
content.value_statement: Developers need reliable patterns for Azure AI Agents that keep conversations, tools, and runs organized. This skill gives focused .NET SDK guidance for agents, threads, streaming, file search, function calling, and cleanup.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to install Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent and Azure.Identity packages.
Explains PersistentAgentsClient setup with DefaultAzureCredential and project endpoints.
Provides patterns for creating agents, threads, messages, runs, and streaming responses.
Covers function calling, tool output submission, file search, Bing grounding, and Azure AI Search.
Documents cleanup steps for threads, agents, vector stores, and uploaded files.
limitations: It is documentation, not an executable project or reusable .NET library.
It assumes an existing Azure AI project, deployed model, and valid service connections.
It does not validate current package versions or service availability at runtime.
It does not include production security controls for user data, tool authorization, or logging.

use_cases.title: Prototype an Agent Workflow
use_cases.description: Create a basic .NET agent flow with a thread, user message, run polling, and response retrieval.

use_cases.title: Add Tool Calling to an Agent
use_cases.description: Use the function calling example to define tool parameters and submit tool outputs during a run.

use_cases.title: Connect Agent Retrieval Sources
use_cases.description: Follow the file search and Azure AI Search sections to plan retrieval-backed assistant behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Agent
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline the minimal .NET steps for creating an Azure AI persistent agent, thread, message, and run.

prompt_templates.title: Add Streaming Responses
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to adapt my polling-based .NET agent flow into a streaming response flow with clear status handling.

prompt_templates.title: Design Function Calling
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a .NET function calling pattern for my agent, including required parameters and tool output submission.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Persistent Agent Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my Azure AI Agents .NET architecture for thread lifecycle, tool resources, streaming, error handling, and cleanup.

output_examples.input: I need a basic persistent agent flow in .NET.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan covering package installation, credential setup, agent creation, thread creation, message creation, run execution, message retrieval, and cleanup.

output_examples.input: Help me add file search to an Azure AI agent.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step explanation of uploading a document, creating a vector store, attaching file search resources, and creating an agent that can search the uploaded content.

output_examples.input: What should I check before publishing my agent service?
output: Verify Azure project endpoint and model deployment names.
Handle run failure and cancellation states.
Restrict tool access and validate function arguments.
Delete temporary threads, files, agents, and vector stores when they are no longer needed.

best_practices: Use managed identity or DefaultAzureCredential instead of hardcoded secrets.
Handle every run status, including required action, failure, cancellation, and expiration.
Clean up agents, threads, uploaded files, and vector stores created for temporary workflows.
anti_patterns: Do not paste Azure credentials, connection strings, or private document content into prompts.
Do not execute tool calls from model output without validating function names and arguments.
Do not leave test agents, files, and vector stores running after experiments finish.

faq.question: What SDK does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers the Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent package for .NET and related Azure Identity authentication patterns.

faq.question: Can it create a complete application for me?
faq.answer: It can guide implementation, but it is not a complete application scaffold or deployed service.

faq.question: Does it support streaming responses?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a streaming run pattern and explains how to handle streaming update types.

faq.question: Does it include retrieval examples?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers file search with vector stores and Azure AI Search tool resources.

faq.question: Does it require Azure resources?
faq.answer: Yes. You need an Azure AI project endpoint, a model deployment, and any required service connections.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file is documentation with low risk from command examples and external reference links.
